packs.title: Keigo Production
packs.description: Practice producing humble and honorific verb forms in workplace contexts

stages.prompt: You're telling your manager you'll do the task. Use the humble form of 'to do' (humble form of する).
stages.hint: いたす (humble form of する)

stages.prompt: You're introducing yourself to a client. Use the humble form of 'to say/be called' (humble form of 言う).

modelAnswer.english: I am called Tanaka. / My name is Tanaka.

stages.hint: 申す (humble form of 言う)

stages.prompt: You're telling a client you'll visit their office tomorrow. Use the humble form of 'to go' (humble form of 行く).

modelAnswer.japanese: 明日そちらに参ります。
modelAnswer.kana: あしたそちらにまいります。

stages.hint: 参る (humble form of 行く)

stages.prompt: You're reporting to your manager that you looked at the document. Use the humble form of 'to look at/read' (humble form of 見る).

modelAnswer.japanese: ドキュメントを拝見しました。
modelAnswer.kana: ドキュメントをはいけんしました。

stages.hint: 拝見する (humble form of 見る)

stages.prompt: You're asking your manager if you can ask them about the schedule. Use the humble form of 'to ask/visit/hear' (multi-purpose humble verb).

modelAnswer.japanese: スケジュールについて伺ってもよろしいでしょうか。
modelAnswer.kana: スケジュールについてうかがってもよろしいでしょうか。
modelAnswer.english: May I ask you about the schedule?

stages.hint: 伺う (humble form of 聞く/訪問する)

packs.title: Keigo Production 2
packs.description: Practice honorific verbs and complex keigo patterns

stages.prompt: You're telling a colleague that the manager is in the office. Use the honorific form of 'to be' (honorific form of いる).

modelAnswer.japanese: 部長はオフィスにいらっしゃいます。
modelAnswer.kana: ぶちょうはオフィスにいらっしゃいます。
modelAnswer.english: The department head is in the office.

stages.hint: いらっしゃる (honorific form of いる/行く/来る)

stages.prompt: You're reporting what the client said in the meeting. Use the honorific form of 'to say' (honorific form of 言う).

modelAnswer.japanese: お客様は了解しましたとおっしゃいました。
modelAnswer.kana: おきゃくさまはりょうかいしましたとおっしゃいました。

stages.hint: おっしゃる (honorific form of 言う)

stages.prompt: You're asking if the manager will do the review. Use the honorific form of 'to do' (honorific form of する).

modelAnswer.japanese: 部長がレビューをなさいますか。
modelAnswer.kana: ぶちょうがレビューをなさいますか。
modelAnswer.english: Will the department head do the review?

stages.hint: なさる (honorific form of する)

stages.prompt: You're thanking the manager for giving you advice. Use the honorific form of 'to give' (honorific form of くれる).

modelAnswer.japanese: アドバイスをくださってありがとうございます。
modelAnswer.kana: アドバイスをくださってありがとうございます。
modelAnswer.english: Thank you for giving me advice.

stages.hint: くださる (honorific form of くれる)

stages.prompt: You're asking the client to please look at the document (complex keigo pattern combining honorific + humble).

modelAnswer.japanese: 資料をご覧いただけますでしょうか。
modelAnswer.kana: しりょうをごらんいただけますでしょうか。
modelAnswer.english: Could you please look at the materials?

stages.hint: ご覧いただく (honorific お/ご + verb stem + いただく)
